title: JIT coverage 1 of 2 — the every-function structural set, so real compiled code reaches the demand path at all
purpose: Ledger A2, first brief. LADDER RECONCILIATION (binding context, folded here per the coordinator's rider rather than side-noted): the hosting-ladder's §0 row 'Cranelift JIT — Landed' (DESIGN.md:51) is true and, post-JIT-001, means RUNTIME-REACHABLE — the profiler heats at both call edges and compilation rides the dirty-CPU service. But ground truth at 41d4535 is that the reachable tier is effectively INERT on real code: the Instruction enum has 75 variants, the pre-pass accepts 47, and the rejected 28 include Line, Allocate/AllocateHeap/AllocateZero, Deallocate, TestHeap, InitYregs, Trim, CallLast, CallExtLast, and ApplyLast (pre-pass catch-all, ir_control.rs:297-301; independently verified at the bytes). Every erlc-emitted function body carries Line; nearly all carry a frame. So the compilable set today is synthetic-leaf-only, and the demand path's unsupported verdict (mark_unsupported → STATE_UNSUPPORTED, terminal within a generation, retried at a newer one — profiler.rs:235-247,343-361) is what actually fires on production-shaped code. JIT-001's boundary declared lowerings out of scope and named A2 as the lane that reopens the supported set (JIT-001.json boundaries; JIT-001.json:147). This brief is that lane's first wave: the STRUCTURAL set that gates whole-function admission. It CONSUMES the post-JIT-001 profiler contract unchanged — generation+epoch dual-key (profiler.rs:44-56), monotonic generations across delete (02e7509) — and changes nothing about it. Sub-op widening (TypeTest beyond 6/17 incl. IsFloat, the Bif import-table reach beyond 5 hard-coded arithmetic slots, Bs*/BinaryOp beyond 13/21, fmove operand forms) is WAVE 2 (the arc's next brief), and exception/error-terminal lowerings ride with it. Base: beamr main at 41d4535 or later.
task: Lower the eleven structural opcodes that gate real-function admission (Line; the frame family Allocate/AllocateHeap/AllocateZero/Deallocate/TestHeap/InitYregs/Trim; the tail-call family CallLast/CallExtLast/ApplyLast), admit CallFun2 to the pre-pass whose lowering already exists dead at dispatch_call.rs:169-203, and replace the two silent catch-alls' implicit knowledge with ONE compile-enforced coverage classification that the pre-pass, the dispatch, and the tests all consume — so a new Instruction variant cannot be silently unclassified and the supported set has a single source of truth. Prove the outcome on a REAL erlc-produced module through the wired demand path, differential-equal to the interpreter. Deviations from this brief — including improvements — STOP and report; they are not improvised.

requirements.title: The every-function structural set — Line + frame family lowered
requirements.spec: Lower, with semantics identical to the interpreter's dispatch arms (interpreter/opcodes/ is the semantic reference, per-opcode): Line (no-op in execution — the interpreter treats it so at opcodes/mod.rs:236; the JIT skips it in both the pre-pass block-structure scan and dispatch); Allocate, AllocateHeap, AllocateZero (stack-frame reservation, with AllocateZero's NIL-initialization and AllocateHeap's heap-need guard honored); Deallocate; TestHeap (heap-need guard); InitYregs (NIL-init of named y registers); Trim (frame window shift). Frame and heap-guard lowerings MUST reuse the existing runtime-helper seam (runtime.rs / safepoint.rs) the way current lowerings reach process state; heap guards compose with the existing GC discipline the same way the interpreter's arms do — the JIT'd code must never grow the heap around the collector. STOP-AND-REPORT TRIPWIRE (binding): if any opcode in this set requires NEW GC, safepoint, or stack-representation machinery — rather than new uses of existing helpers — stop and report with the blocking fact; that is an architecture decision above this brief, and 'a helper that almost fits, adjusted' counts as new machinery for this clause. Y-SUBSTRATE RULING (domain seat, 2026-07-22, resolving the build leg's first STOP-AND-REPORT — the tripwire working as designed): the JIT's flat Y addressing (ir_common.rs:161 register_offset, Y(i) → X_REGISTER_COUNT + i) is a LATENT HAZARD, verified at the bytes: call_native hands native code the x_regs buffer alone (opcodes/core.rs:835-841, 1024 slots, no Y space) and the collector roots Y registers exclusively from process.stack().y_regs() (process/mod.rs:576) — flat-Y writes are simultaneously out-of-bounds of the register file and invisible to GC, unreachable today only because Allocate is rejected. R1 therefore lands Y access on the process's CANONICAL stack: new extern-C helper wrappers over the EXISTING push_frame/pop_frame/y_reg/set_y_reg/trim_y_regs and gc::ensure_space primitives, reached through the *mut Process pointer native code already receives. This is new USES of existing machinery — the sanctioned side of the tripwire; the stack representation, GC rooting, and safepoint discipline are all reused unchanged. The flat Y addressing is REMOVED in the same change — no dual Y substrate survives this brief — and the landed tests that pinned flat-Y behavior (including compiled_try_catches_interpreted_exception_and_exposes_payload, compiler_tests.rs:2171, which exercises the already-supported Try/TryCase Y path frameless) re-pin onto the stack-backed substrate as part of R1, named here so the re-pin is brief-mandated rather than improvised.
acceptance: Per-opcode compiled_* walls in compiler_tests.rs for each of the seven non-trivial opcodes (Line, the eighth, needs only the admission proof below), matching the existing per-opcode wall style
The flat Y addressing path is gone: no Y-register arithmetic against X_REGISTER_COUNT survives in ir_common.rs (grep-provable), and the re-pinned flat-Y tests are green on the stack-backed substrate
A function containing the full set (frame allocate → y-reg use → trim → deallocate) compiles and its JIT execution is value-identical to interpreter execution on the same inputs
Heap-guard behavior pinned: a TestHeap/AllocateHeap function whose guard must trip still produces correct results through the JIT path (no silent heap overrun; observed via a heap-pressure fixture)
No new #[allow]; no unwrap/expect/panic outside tests

requirements.title: Tail calls — CallLast, CallExtLast, ApplyLast
requirements.spec: Lower the three Last-form calls with true tail semantics: frame teardown (the Deallocate component rides R1's mechanism) followed by transfer that composes with the existing call lowerings' dispatch shape (dispatch_call.rs's Call/CallExt/Apply arms are the semantic base). The JIT boundary contract holds: where the existing non-last call lowerings return control to the trampoline/edge rather than native-inlining the callee, the Last forms do the same — a tail call from JIT'd code MUST NOT grow native stack per iteration. A self-recursive tail loop is the acceptance instrument: it must run a depth that would overflow any per-call native stack growth (1e6 iterations minimum) with flat memory.
acceptance: compiled_* walls for all three opcodes

Tail-recursion flatness pin: 1e6-deep self tail-recursion through the JIT path completes with results equal to the interpreter's and no stack growth (the pin fails if a per-iteration native frame sneaks in)
CallExtLast exercised against a genuinely external callee (cross-module fixture), not just a same-module alias

requirements.title: CallFun2 pre-pass admission — the dead lowering wired, and the class of defect walled
requirements.spec: Admit CallFun2 to the pre-pass (ir_control.rs:230 currently reads Apply | CallFun only) so its existing, complete lowering (dispatch_call.rs:169-203) becomes reachable. Then wall the DEFECT CLASS, not just the instance: a test that derives the pre-pass-accepted set and the dispatch-handled set (via R4's classification table once it exists — see R4's consistency clause) and fails on any variant the dispatch can lower but the pre-pass rejects, or vice versa. The two catch-alls made this divergence silent; after this brief it is structurally loud. TAIL-ONLY CALL MODEL RULING (2026-07-22, build-leg STOP #3, byte-verified at the domain seat): every helper-return call lowering — CallExt/CallExtOnly/Apply/CallFun/CallFun2 — terminates the compiled function and returns the callee's result to the edge (dispatch_helpers.rs handle_helper_return writes X0 then return_status unconditionally, :143-149; probe: [CallExt echo, Move 777→X0, Return] returns the callee's result, not 777), and local plain Call lowers as a bare in-slice jump (dispatch_call.rs:216-228). The tier has NO body-call model: a body-position call's continuation is silently dropped. Latent in the baseline (real erlc body calls always carry a frame and the frame family was rejected); ARMED by R1 exactly as the flat-Y hazard was — a single-clause frame-plus-body-call erlc function slips the slicer and mis-executes. MANDATORY, in scope: the pre-pass gains a STRUCTURAL TAIL-POSITION WALL — CallExt, Apply, CallFun, CallFun2, and plain Call are admitted only when immediately followed by Return; the *Last/*Only forms are inherently tail and unaffected; violation rejects the whole function into the normal mark_unsupported/interpreter fallback. Fail-first: the 777 probe as the wall's red test, plus a demand-path test proving a body-call erlc-shaped module falls back to the interpreter and returns the interpreter's answer. The R4 table stays PER-OPCODE (its rustdoc gains: Supported = lowerable under the tier's tail-only call model; body-position structure is the pre-pass wall's business, like label resolution — count stays 59). Existing R1/R2 synthetic walls reshape to wall-legal forms where they used body-position calls (Y-liveness is provable via GcBif/TestHeap pressure without body calls). The CONTINUATION CALL MODEL (native CP, return-into-JIT code) is successor-arc business, deferred with provenance to the real-erlc-admission brief named in R5's SLICER RULING. BIF NO-FAIL RULING (2026-07-22, build-leg STOP #4, byte-verified at the domain seat): erlc emits fail label {f,0} — the no-local-handler sentinel — on ALL body-position arithmetic, and both the Bif pre-pass (ir_control.rs ensure_known_label on parsed.fail) and the lowering (dispatch_core.rs blocks.label_block) treat Label(0) as a missing label, so no real-erlc arithmetic-bearing function can compile. RULED: fail == Label(0) routes to the EXISTING deopt block (new use of existing machinery — the typed-int overflow path already takes blocks.deopt on the same instruction shape), UNDER A STRUCTURAL PURITY GUARD, because deopt semantics are restart-the-callee-interpreted-from-start (JIT_STATUS_DEOPT → Ok(None) → ordinary bytecode call path, core.rs:850): the pre-pass admits a {f,0}-carrying Bif only when NO observable-side-effect instruction precedes it in the slice (the side-effect set — Send and any mailbox/receive-family opcode — is DERIVED from the R4 table, not hand-listed), so any deopt-restart re-executes only pure code. Wave-2's import-table widening boundary is untouched (slot set unchanged; this rules label handling, not new BIFs). Fail-first: the countdown fixture red at UnknownLabel{0} before, compiling after; a badarith case through the deopt path returning interpreter-equal exception behavior; and a wall test that a {f,0} Bif AFTER a Send is rejected. NOTED FOR THE SUCCESSOR ARC, not fixed here: the pre-existing typed-overflow deopt edge carries the same restart class (reachable only via contrived literal-overflow-after-send today); proper deopt metadata is the admission arc's business.
acceptance: A CallFun2-bearing function compiles and runs JIT with results equal to the interpreter's

The pre-pass/dispatch consistency wall exists, passes, and FAILS if either side is modified without the other (fail-first: re-introduce the CallFun2 omission and observe the wall go red; recorded in the handoff, not committed)

The tail-position wall rejects every body-position CallExt/Apply/CallFun/CallFun2/Call (777-probe red test at the wall; demand-path fallback test returns interpreter-equal results); tail-shaped baseline pins stay green

{f,0} Bifs compile to the deopt route under the purity guard: countdown fixture red-then-green at UnknownLabel{0}; badarith-through-deopt interpreter-equal; {f,0}-Bif-after-Send rejected (guard wall test); side-effect set derived from the R4 table

requirements.title: Coverage source of truth — one classification, compile-enforced, consumed everywhere
requirements.spec: Introduce a single classification of every Instruction variant into Supported | RejectedIncremental { reason } | RejectedInherent { reason } (naming is the builder's call within house style), living in the jit module beside the pre-pass. The classifying match is EXHAUSTIVE WITH NO WILDCARD ARM — adding an Instruction variant must break compilation until the variant is classified (this is the wall the two silent catch-alls never gave us; new-variant drift today falls through both of them unnoticed, and no #[deny]/exhaustiveness guard exists anywhere in the compiler — verified at the bytes). The pre-pass catch-all and the dispatch catch-all both keep their UnsupportedOpcode returns but the classification becomes the documented authority they must agree with: a consistency test walks all 75 variants and asserts (a) every Supported variant is pre-pass-accepted, (b) every Rejected variant is pre-pass-rejected, (c) the R3 pre-pass/dispatch wall holds through the same walk. INHERENT classifications at this head: FuncInfo (structural prelude, stripped by the slicer — aot.rs:352), OnLoad and NifStart (module-lifecycle pseudo-ops, not steady-state execution; NifStart is not even interpreter-dispatched — opcodes/mod.rs:461), Generic (unknown-by-construction). Everything else rejected at this head is RejectedIncremental with the wave that owns it named in the reason (wave-2 items per the purpose). The classification's rustdoc states it is the coverage table of record and that JIT-002/JIT-003 move variants only by editing it.
acceptance: Exhaustiveness proof: the classifying match has no wildcard arm (grep-level check in review) — a mock new variant breaking compilation is demonstrated in the handoff, not committed
The 75-variant consistency walk passes and is 10x stable

Post-R1/R2/R3 the Supported count is exactly 47 + 12 = 59 (R1's eight incl. Line + R2's three + CallFun2 — count corrected by the build leg's Finding A, 2026-07-22; the original 58 omitted Line from the delta) and the walk derives that count from the table, not a hard-coded literal duplicated in the test

requirements.title: Real-function acceptance — an erlc module JITs end-to-end, and the unsupported-representative re-pin
requirements.spec: The proof this brief changed reality rather than counts: a fixture module produced by REAL erlc (OTP-29, the house fixture toolchain; committed .beam with its .erl source beside it, probe-workload precedent) whose exported function is a real compiled-Erlang tail-recursive loop written as an explicitly ?MODULE-qualified self-call with Line instructions throughout (shape trued by the SLICER RULING and its STOP #3 amendment below; the frame/Y-across-call/CallExtLast clauses are deferred to the successor real-erlc-admission brief) — heats through record_call at the wired edges, compiles on the dirty-CPU service, dispatches native, and produces results equal to the same module run under a minimal (JIT-absent) composition. Consume public API only (first-external-consumer doctrine). Bounded waits on the JIT-001 telemetry counters, never bare sleeps. RE-PIN RIDER (binding): jit_wireup.rs:582's unsupported_function_retries_at_the_new_generation currently uses Trim as its unsupported representative; R1 makes Trim supported, so that test's premise dies. Re-pin it on a variant the R4 table still classifies RejectedIncremental (Catch or UpdateRecord are the natural picks), and derive the representative's rejected-ness FROM the table in the test setup — so wave 2 flipping that variant breaks the test loudly at the table lookup instead of silently hollowing the retry proof. SLICER RULING (2026-07-22, build-leg STOP #2, ruled at the domain seat after byte-verification): the JIT-001/AOT slicer (Module::function_instructions and aot.rs exported_instructions, aot.rs:351-355) starts the instruction slice at entry+1, stripping the entry Label — so a LOCAL self-tail CallLast targeting the entry label dies at UnknownLabel before any JIT-002 code runs. That slicer is JIT-001 machinery bound by the AOT slice-equality pin and is OUT of this brief's scope (the boundary already forbids modifying consumed JIT-001 contracts). Therefore the fixture's tail-recursive loop MUST be written as an explicitly ?MODULE-qualified self-call, which erlc compiles to call_ext_last (external calls must honor code reloading; the compiler cannot localize them) — a genuine real-erlc tail-recursive loop that exercises R2's CallExtLast lowering end-to-end while dodging the stripped label. Verify at erlc -S output that the loop compiles to call_ext_last before committing the fixture, and carry a comment in the .erl naming this gate. The LOCAL-label CallLast self-recursion end-to-end proof is DEFERRED with provenance to a JIT-001-machinery slicer brief (entry-label retention under the slice-equality pin; wave-pool candidate beside JIT-003); the build leg's archived jit_real_function.{erl,beam} (artemis-artifacts/2026-07-22-jit002build/) is that brief's ready-made red fixture, and differential.rs:1128's skipped fibonacci test remains the in-tree acknowledged marker. SLICER RULING AMENDED (STOP #3, same day): the gate is broader than self-recursion — the slicer also strips the func_info prelude label that multi-clause dispatch fail-edges target (select_val → UnknownLabel), and under R3's tail-only wall real erlc can NEVER combine frames/Y-across-call with an admissible body call (erlc emits allocate only to spill across body calls). The 'frame allocation, y-register use across a call' and CallExtLast clauses of this requirement are therefore UNSATISFIABLE on real erlc this wave: they are proven at the compiler level by R1/R2's synthetic walls and their real-erlc end-to-end rides the successor REAL-ERLC-ADMISSION brief (one coherent arc: continuation call model with native CP + slicer entry/prelude-label retention; wave-pool candidate beside JIT-003 and the monitor/2 boxed-ref fix; the build leg's three archived fixtures in artemis-artifacts/2026-07-22-jit002build/ are its red suite). The fixture this brief commits is trued to the honestly-provable shape: a real-erlc TWO-CLAUSE tail-recursive loop whose second clause is a variable catch-all (no func_info fail edge), self-call ?MODULE-qualified — frameless, so erlc emits call_ext_only (verify at erlc -S; the import-table tail transfer is the thing proven live) — Line instructions throughout, arithmetic via the admitted Bif slots, heated through record_call, compiled on the dirty-CPU service, dispatched native, result-equal to the minimal composition. If even that shape hits an unruled rejection, STOP again rather than thin the fixture further. (STOP #4 resolved the first such rejection: body-position arithmetic's {f,0} sentinel, ruled in R3's BIF NO-FAIL RULING — the countdown's pure prefix satisfies the purity guard, so the ruled shape compiles.)
acceptance: erlc fixture (committed .beam + .erl) compiles through the demand path and dispatches native: counter-verified (1 submission, 1 success), result-equal to the minimal-composition run, 10x stable; the loop is a ?MODULE-qualified self-call verified call_ext_only at erlc -S per the amended SLICER RULING (frame/Y/CallExtLast and local-label CallLast end-to-end deferred to the real-erlc-admission successor brief, provenance in spec)

unsupported_function_retries_at_the_new_generation green with a table-derived representative; fail-first evidence that the test reddens when the representative is classified Supported (handoff, not committed)
All 16 existing jit_wireup acceptance tests remain green unmodified except the re-pin

create: crates/beamr/tests/fixtures/jit_real_function.erl (name is builder's call; .beam committed beside it)

requirements.title: Full gate bar on the final head
requirements.spec: The composition-campaign bar, unchanged: cargo fmt --check; cargo check -p beamr; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings AND --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test -p beamr --lib; cargo test -p beamr --test '*'; 10x stability loops on every new pin. Zero new #[allow] anywhere. No unwrap/expect/panic outside tests. wasm32 check unaffected (beamr-wasm builds; the jit feature is not in its graph — verify, don't assume). Handoff pack states toolchain versions measured on the final head.
acceptance: All gates exit 0 on the final head, toolchain-stamped
grep proves zero new #[allow] in the diff
beamr-wasm wasm32 build exits 0 at the final head

boundaries: Wave 2 (the arc's next brief) owns ALL sub-op widening: TypeTest beyond the supported 6 (incl. IsFloat, ir_control_validation.rs:36), the Bif import-table reach beyond the 5 hard-coded arithmetic slots (ir_arithmetic.rs:36), Bif0/Bif1/GcBif1/GcBif3 (ir_arithmetic.rs:98), Bs*/BinaryOp widening (ir_binary.rs:150), fmove operand forms — OUT here

Exception-machinery and error-terminal lowerings (Catch, CatchEnd, TryCaseEnd, Badmatch, Badrecord, CaseEnd, IfEnd, Raise, RawRaise, BuildStacktrace), SelectTupleArity, and UpdateRecord are OUT — wave 2 pool; R4 classifies them RejectedIncremental with the wave named
The INHERENT set stays rejected BY DESIGN: FuncInfo, OnLoad, NifStart, Generic — R4 records why; no lowering attempts
The profiler/fallback contract is CONSUMED, not modified: no changes to generation/epoch semantics, mark_* signatures, or retry behavior — JIT-001's machinery is the platform, not the patient

No emitted-.beam-bytes or encode-surface changes of any kind (standing cross-repo assurance to aion's BC-4 differential harness, 2026-07-22); no loader changes; no interpreter semantic changes — the interpreter is the semantic REFERENCE for every lowering, never the modified party
No new services, threads, or inventory lines; the operand-range try_from guards (the ~70 INHERENT robustness bounds) are boundaries of supported lowerings and stay exactly as they are
The ledger's A1-2 (CLI .beamr_native companion loading) remains its own future brief — untouched here
verification: Full R6 bar on the final head. The acceptance spine is R5's real-function end-to-end (10x, counter-verified, minimal-composition differential) + R4's 75-variant consistency walk + R2's tail-flatness pin. Fail-first evidence where each R names it (R3 wall, R5 re-pin), recorded in the handoff. Reviewer-of-record per house convention; Sol pre-review of the folded brief text against pinned bytes before the build leg (house standard), norn adversarial rounds when norn returns to service, Fable tear at the domain seat before declaration to the coordinator's tear.
